moves.thinking:
In Kuhn Poker with a K card, I have the strongest possible hand. Let me think about the optimal strategy.

With a K:
- If I check and opponent checks, we go to showdown where I win (K beats everything)
- If I check and opponent bets, I can call and win at showdown
- If I bet, opponent might fold (I win the pot immediately) or call (I win at showdown since K is highest)

The Nash equilibrium strategy in Kuhn Poker:
- With K: Always bet (or check-raise if possible)
- With Q: Sometimes bet, sometimes check
- With J: Sometimes bluff, sometimes check

With the strongest card K, betting is the optimal play because:
1. I extract value from weaker hands that might call
2. I don't risk giving a free card to opponent who might have Q or J

If I check:
- Opponent with Q might check behind, and I only win 2 chips
- Opponent with J might check behind, and I only win 2 chips
- Opponent might bet, I call, and win 4 chips

If I bet:
- Opponent with Q might call, I win 4 chips
- Opponent with J might fold (I win 2 chips) or call as a bluff-catch (I win 4 chips)
- Either way, I'm getting value

With K, betting is clearly optimal to maximize expected value. The Nash equilibrium has player betting with K 100% of the time.
moves.prompt:
You are playing 2-player Kuhn Poker. The deck has three cards: J, Q, K (ranked J < Q < K). Each player is dealt one private card and antes 1 chip. On your turn you choose one action:
  - check: pass without betting (only when no bet is facing you)
  - bet: put in 1 more chip
  - call: match a facing bet (go to showdown)
  - fold: give up the pot
At showdown the higher card wins the pot. Play to maximize your chips.

Before you act, consider your card's strength and what the betting so far suggests about your opponent.

Match: Hand 2 of 8.
You are player_0 in Kuhn Poker. Your private card is K (ranks: J<Q<K). Pot is 2 chips. Action history: (no actions yet). Legal actions: check, bet.

Choose exactly one of these legal actions: check, bet.
Respond with ONLY the single action word, nothing else.
